summary: Parallel review: FAIL (4 critical, 4 warning, 13 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: - `mergeReviewFindings` (flows.mjs:177–202) correctly relabels simplicity 🔴 as `[simplicity veto]`, preserves 🟡/🔵 as `[simplicity]`

🔴 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:60 — Add a test that when a role returns `{ok: false, output: ""}` the merged result still surfaces a critical/warning finding; today a crashed reviewer is indistinguishable from a clean one at run.mjs:1196-1199.

🔴 bin/lib/run.mjs:360 — `output: result.output || ""` swallows reviewer failures. Inject a synthetic finding when `result.ok === false`, or have `mergeReviewFindings` count failed roles and fail-closed; otherwise build-verify can ship green when a reviewer never ran.

🔴 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:42 — No assertion that the 6 reviews run concurrently. Add a stub-based test (mock `dispatchToAgentAsync` to record start order vs resolve order) so a regression to sequential execution is caught.
🟡 test/build-verify-parallel-review.test.mjs:42 — `runParallelReviews` is not asserted to forward `cwd` to each role's dispatch; add a test that all 6 calls receive the same `cwd`.
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1198 — `roleFindings.map(f => f.output||"").join("\n")` re-parses what `mergeReviewFindings` just parsed; drift between eval.md and synthesized verdict would not be caught.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:336 — `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` runs all 6 parallel reviewers with full tool access; combined with raw `gateOutput` embedded in the brief (flows.mjs:100), this is a latent prompt-injection vector via crafted dependency test output. Pre-existing — track in backlog.
🟡 bin/lib/flows.mjs:34 — Phases `review` and `multi-review` now drive identical behavior; track follow-up to collapse to a single phase name so the FLOWS contract reflects what actually runs.
